articles.title: Key magic mushroom ingredient makes fish less aggressive and lazier
articles.description: More than 200 mushrooms—primarily those belonging to a genus of gilled mushrooms called Psilocybe—contain the psychoactive compound psilocybin. In the brain of mammals, this chemical can bind to serotonin receptors and influence behavior and emotions, including aggression, appetite, and mood. Its effects on the social behavior of animals, however, remain largely undescribed.

articles.description: Whooping cough, medically known as pertussis, is a highly contagious bacterial infection that affects the airways. It gets its common name from the "whoop" sound that some infected children make when they take a deep, gasping breath after a severe coughing fit.

articles.title: Cognitive behavioral therapy shows promise managing menopausal insomnia and hot flashes
articles.description: Insomnia is a common problem, affecting as many as 20% to 60% of perimenopausal and postmenopausal US women. Because of its possible severe physical and psychological effects, ongoing research is focused on identifying effective treatment options. A new study suggests that cognitive behavioral therapy intervention may produce at least short-term benefits. Results of the study are published in Menopause.

articles.title: Argentina races to find origins of cruise ship hantavirus outbreak, amid reports some passengers have returned to US
articles.description:
Argentina, where the MV Hondius cruise departed, consistently ranked by WHO as having highest incidence of hantavirus in region
Officials and experts in Argentina are scrambling to determine if their country is the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak that has gripped an Atlantic cruise ship, amid reports that a number of passengers have already returned to their home countries.
Argentina, where the cruise to Antarctica departed, is consistently ranked by the World Health Organization (WHO) as having the highest incidence of the rare, rodent-borne disease in Latin America. Investigators there are working to contact trace the source of contamination.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Stereotypes of autism in TV and film may be linked to delayed diagnosis, study finds
articles.description: Stereotypes of autistic men in films and TV programs may contribute to delayed diagnosis of autistic women and non-binary people, a new University of Stirling study has found. Research conducted by a group of autistic and non-autistic researchers, led by Sarah Dantas of the University's Faculty of Natural Sciences, has found that women and non-binary people may experience delayed diagnosis because dominant images of autism in the media don't match their own experiences.

Volatility also decreased, indicating reduced near-term risk.

articles.title: Scientists finally solve 40-year-old physics puzzle about how things grow
articles.description: In a major breakthrough, scientists have experimentally confirmed a universal growth law in two dimensions using a quantum system of fleeting light–matter particles. articles.title: Musk’s biggest loyalist became his biggest liability
articles.description:
I sat down in the Musk v. Altman trial courtroom today, painfully aware that no one was going to ask Shivon Zilis the question on everyone's minds: Girl, what the fuck are you doing?
Zilis, who testified under oath that she is the mother of four of Musk's children, was… what's the best way to characterize this? A Musk advisor? She denies she was a "chief of staff" but says she worked for Musk's "entire AI portfolio: Tesla, Neuralink, and OpenAI" starting in 2017. The two met through OpenAI, and they had what she referred to as a "one off" before becoming "friends and colleagues." The "one off," she confirmed, was "romantic in nature."
Her  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: This town found clean energy deep inside old coal mines
articles.description: Cumberland, B.C. is reimagining its coal mining past as a clean energy opportunity. Water trapped in abandoned mine tunnels could be used in a geothermal system to heat and cool buildings efficiently and with minimal emissions. The project could lower energy costs, support new development, and attract businesses. It’s a striking example of turning industrial leftovers into a sustainable community asset.

articles.title: NASA captures wild swirling clouds and rare arctic storm over Alaska
articles.description: Southern Alaska’s winter finale delivered a spectacular atmospheric display, captured by a NASA satellite. Cold Arctic air flowing over warmer ocean waters created long bands of clouds, swirling vortex patterns, and even a compact polar storm with powerful winds. As the air traveled offshore, it evolved into increasingly complex cloud formations. The result was a dramatic, ever-changing sky that highlighted the raw energy of the season’s end.

articles.title: Nintendo announces a new Star Fox for the Switch 2
articles.description:
It turns out Fox McCloud's appearance in the Super Mario Galaxy Movie was a tease of things to come: Nintendo just surprise announced the first new Star Fox game in a decade. The game is called, simply, Star Fox, and it's out very soon, launching on June 25th.
Nintendo says that it's "based" on the classic space shooter Star Fox 64, but with redesigned characters and upgraded visuals. Essentially, it sounds like a modern remake of the game with the same level design. Based on the first look at the game, it looks thoroughly modern, with incredibly detailed characters, including a surprisingly realistic Slippy Toad. And yes, the in-ship bante …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari' PA shot dead LIVE: Contract killers involved? Police launch massive hunt, say ‘Glock 47X pistols used’
articles.description: The murder of Chandranath Rath, aide to BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari, ignited political tensions in West Bengal. The BJP accused TMC of fostering lawlessness after Rath was shot in Madhyamgram, prompting police investigations into the attack and possible involvement of hired killers.

articles.title: West Bengal: Post-poll tension heightens after Suvendu Adhikari’s aide shot dead; police deployment increased
articles.description: Chandranath Rath, a former IAF employee, was shot dead by motorcycle-borne assailants who tailed his car before intercepting it near Doharia in Madhyamgram.

articles.title: In just four months of 2026, Tamil Nadu records 2.63 lakh dog bites, 17 deaths
articles.description: A total of 62,000 dog bite cases were recorded each in January and February, and it rose to 71,000 in March and dipping slightly to 68,000 in April; half of last year’s deaths has been recorded in just four months this year

articles.title: 'Good head on his shoulders' - Connolly's happy IPL with PBKS continues
articles.description: Sairaj Bahutule credits Cooper Connolly, who scored an unbeaten 107 in 59 balls, for not giving up even when the chase against SRH was dead in the water

articles.title: Ex-IAF employee to Suvendu Adhikari's ‘Mr dependable’ PA, who was Chandranath Rath?
articles.description: Chandranath Rath reportedly took voluntary retirement from the Air Force, following which he moved to the corporate sector for a brief period.

articles.title: Who was Chandranath Rath, an ex-IAF serviceman who became Suvendu Adhikari's trusted aide?
articles.description: Chandranath Rath, 41, hailed from Chandipur in Purba Medinipur district, the political turf that shaped Adhikari's rise in Bengal politics, party sources were quoted as saying by news agency PTI.

articles.title: One year of Operation Sindhoor | PM Modi says Indian forces gave fitting response to terrorism
articles.description: PM Modi said India remains steadfast in its resolve to defeat terrorism

articles.title: 'Will wait for Vijay's TVK to form govt in Tamil Nadu and watch for 6 months': Outgoing CM Stalin
articles.description: Vijay's TVK pulled off a stunning show in the recently concluded Tamil Nadu poll in its debut poll, winning 108 seats — short of just 10 from the majority mark.

articles.title: 'Just took 88 hours': Army shares video revisiting Operation Sindoor as it completes one year
articles.description: The Indian armed forces shared a throwback montage video, posting it exactly at 1:05 am – the precise time the mission was launched on May 7 last year.

topStories.title: Mbappé Will Power France to World Cup Gold Over Spain, BofA Says
topStories.description: France will win this year’s FIFA World Cup and claim its third title by beating Spain in the final, Bank of America Corp. analysts predicted.

topStories.title: Oil and Stocks Depend on How Serious Trump Deal Is
topStories.description: Markets care far more about the Strait than Iran’s nuclear program.

topStories.title: War and Energy Shortages Boost China’s Influence in Asia
topStories.description: The war in Iran has left China’s neighbors appealing for help, handing Beijing the kind of sway it has long sought.

topStories.title: Supreme Court examines validity of excluding CJI from Election Commission selection panel
topStories.description: Supreme Court hears challenge to 2023 law on Election Commission appointments, examines validity of excluding CJI from selection panel.

topStories.title: Silver rockets Rs 4,000/kg, Gold near Rs 1.53 lakh/10g as Iran war peace talk optimism sparks rally. What’s next for investors?
topStories.description: Gold and silver prices rose on MCX on Thursday, aided by a weaker dollar and optimism over a potential US-Iran peace deal. Iran said it is reviewing a US proposal to end the conflict, though key issues around its nuclear programme and the Strait of Hormuz remain unresolved.

topStories.title: This 23-Year-Old Woman Built An Airbnb Where You Wake Up Next To A Giraffe
topStories.description: A 23-year-old animal lover has created a rare Airbnb-style stay in Oklahoma where guests can sleep beside a giraffe, drawing visitors from across the US after going viral on TikTok.

topStories.title: Question on state’s sanctuary law turned into personal jabs during California gubernatorial debate
topStories.description: In the race to become California’s next governor, candidates were asked about the state’s law restricting local authorities from cooperating with federal law enforcement, with remarks at times becoming heated between the participants.

topStories.title: Trump threatens Iran strikes, says it's too soon for new direct talks
topStories.description: Trump threatens Iran with "higher level" strikes if it won't accept a peace deal, but says it's too soon for direct talks after reporting diplomatic progress.

topStories.description: Health officials work to contain deadly hantavirus outbreak on cruise; President Trump threatens Iran with more attacks if it doesn't agree to a deal.

topStories.description: The benchmark Nikkei 225 surged over 5% to a record high as Japan reopened after extended Golden Week holidays.

dailyNews.description: Draymond Green joined "Inside the NBA" and roasted Charles Barkley's Houston Rockets era after Barkley declared the Warriors dynasty officially over.

dailyNews.description: US State Department spokesperson says Hezbollah is ‘trying to derail talks’ with Israel.

dailyNews.title: 3 evacuated from hantavirus cruise as Spain says it will dock in Tenerife
dailyNews.description: Three passengers have been evacuated from a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak, as related cases are confirmed in Switzerland and South Africa.

dailyNews.title: Cognitive behavioral therapy shows promise managing menopausal insomnia and hot flashes
dailyNews.description: Insomnia is a common problem, affecting as many as 20% to 60% of perimenopausal and postmenopausal US women. Because of its possible severe physical and psychological effects, ongoing research is focused on identifying effective treatment options. A new study suggests that cognitive behavioral therapy intervention may produce at least short-term benefits. Results of the study are published in Menopause.

dailyNews.title: Former Tennessee head coach Derek Dooley running for U.S. Senate in Georgia's midterm elections
dailyNews.description: Derek Dooley plans to be "the last man standing" in Georgia's Senate race, trading his coaching headset for the political arena in 2026 midterms.

dailyNews.title: Argentina races to find origins of cruise ship hantavirus outbreak, amid reports some passengers have returned to US
dailyNews.description:
Argentina, where the MV Hondius cruise departed, consistently ranked by WHO as having highest incidence of hantavirus in region
Officials and experts in Argentina are scrambling to determine if their country is the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak that has gripped an Atlantic cruise ship, amid reports that a number of passengers have already returned to their home countries.
Argentina, where the cruise to Antarctica departed, is consistently ranked by the World Health Organization (WHO) as having the highest incidence of the rare, rodent-borne disease in Latin America. Investigators there are working to contact trace the source of contamination.
 Continue reading...

dailyNews.title: Backlash erupts over viral video of CAIR leader as Newsom funding draws scrutiny
dailyNews.description: A viral video appears to show CAIR-CA director Zahra Billoo urging supporters to hide hatred strategically, drawing backlash from political figures.

dailyNews.title: Judge denies request to return 2020 ballots seized from Fulton County, Georgia
dailyNews.description: The FBI’s seizure of Fulton County’s voting materials has stoked alarm among election officials and democracy advocates.

dailyNews.title: Julianne Hough rocks leopard-print and snakeskin bikinis during tropical getaway in Fiji
dailyNews.description: Julianne Hough embraced island life in Fiji, sharing photos and videos of herself in skimpy bikinis during her tropical getaway she called "Fiji Dreams."

dailyNews.title: 'Time to say goodbye,' Epstein allegedly wrote in purported suicide note
dailyNews.description: A newly released suicide note purportedly left behind in his jail cell by Jeffrey Epstein before his first attempt to kill himself said, "Time to say goodbye."

topStories.description: The administration’s latest shifts on the status of the conflict show how treacherous it is to speak for a president who cultivates an erratic style.

topStories.title: Trump optimistic about potential deal with Iran
topStories.description: President Trump said he was optimistic about a deal with Iran, sending stocks soaring and oil prices plummeting. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.

topStories.title: Trump’s Indiana Victory Shows His Enduring Grip on Core Republican Supporters
topStories.description: Low approval ratings? MAGA divisions? The president was able to turn out party loyalists in an Indiana primary to help him oust Republican state lawmakers who had crossed him.

topStories.description: New surveillance images show the moment a suspect allegedly opened fire on Secret Service agents near the White House after Vance's motorcade passed by.

topStories.title: How the Fight Over Israel Is Playing Out Inside MAGA
topStories.description: The war in Iran has added to a tectonic shift in public opinion — a bipartisan swing away from Israel. Some on the far-right are fighting to keep President Trump’s movement aligned with the Jewish state.

dailyNews.title: Judge Says F.B.I. Can Keep 2020 Election Records Seized From Georgia
dailyNews.description: The ruling is the latest in a saga driven by President Trump’s desire for redemption in the state, which he lost in 2020. The county is likely to appeal.

dailyNews.title: Jack Smith Calls the Justice Dept. ‘Corrupted’ by Trump and His Allies
dailyNews.description: At a private event in Washington last month, Mr. Smith, the former special counsel, accused Justice Department leaders of targeting people for prosecution to please and impress the president.

dailyNews.title: Trump pauses Project Freedom, calls Iran conflict a "skirmish"
dailyNews.description: President Trump touted U.S. actions against Iran on Wednesday. The comments came one day after he paused the Project Freedom initiative to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz to allow peace negotiations to move forward. CBS News' Holly Williams reports, and Samuel Helfont, an associate professor of strategy and policy at the Naval War College, has more.

dailyNews.title: Todd Blanche says claims of Justice Department politicization are "simply false"
dailyNews.description: Critics have expressed concerns about President Trump's use of the Justice Department, including former President Barack Obama during a taped interview on "The Late Show with Stephen Colbert." CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez sat down for an exclusive interview with acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, who defended the Justice Department's actions.

dailyNews.title: Republicans propose $1 billion in taxpayer money for Trump's ballroom
dailyNews.description: NBC News' Peter Nicholas joins "Here's the Scoop" to report on a new reconciliation bill proposed by Republicans that would allocate $1 billion in taxpayer money to go towards “security adjustments and upgrades" for President Trump's ballroom and East Wing renovation.

dailyNews.title: Indiana primaries show the political cost of crossing Trump: ANALYSIS
dailyNews.description: The message from Indiana’s Republican primaries was hard to miss: if you cross Trump -- even in a state legislative race -- he’s willing to come after you politically.

dailyNews.title: After Trump's attacks on court, Gorsuch determined to be 'fearless' and 'independent'
dailyNews.description: Supreme Court Justice Neil Gorsuch responded to attacks by President Trump in an interview, suggesting he is determined to remain “independent” and “fearless.”

dailyNews.title: Trump asks judges to pause E. Jean Carroll ruling so he can appeal case to SCOTUS
dailyNews.description: President Trump has asked a court to pause its ruling rejecting his challenge to E. Jean Carroll's defamation suit so he can appeal the case to the Supreme Court.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.
